posts.title: Unlock Incredible Savings with Trolleys Supermarket's April Offer Week!
posts.title_ar: عروض أسبوع أبريل المذهلة في تروليز سوبر ماركت!
posts.excerpt: Get ready for a week of unbeatable savings at Trolleys Supermarket UAE! Our "April Offer Week" brings you fantastic discounts on all your grocery essentials and more.
posts.excerpt_ar: استعدوا لأسبوع من التوفير الذي لا يُضاهى في تروليز سوبر ماركت بالإمارات! يقدم لكم "أسبوع عروض أبريل" خصومات رائعة على جميع احتياجاتكم الأساسية والمزيد.
posts.content:
Get ready to fill your carts and save big with Trolleys Supermarket UAE's much-anticipated "April Offer Week"! From April 23rd to April 30th, 2026, we're bringing you an incredible selection of UAE supermarket offers on fresh produce, pantry staples, household items, and much more. Don't miss out on these fantastic weekly discounts UAE designed to make your grocery shopping more affordable and enjoyable.

Whether you're in Mirdif Dubai, Al Taawun Sharjah, Al Khan Sharjah, or Al Nuaimia Ajman, our doors are open to help you discover amazing Dubai grocery deals. Our dedicated team has curated a catalog packed with value, ensuring you get the best for less. Visit your nearest Trolleys Supermarket branch today and experience the difference of quality and savings!

Q: What are the dates for Trolleys Supermarket's April Offer Week?
A: The April Offer Week is valid from April 23rd to April 30th, 2026, across all our UAE branches.

Q: Where can I find the April Offer Week catalog?
A: You can pick up a physical catalog at any of our branches or check our social media channels for digital updates.

Join our WhatsApp Channel: https://whatsapp.com/channel/0029VbBzYPDA2pL8dOLkNl2p
posts.content_ar:
استعدوا لملء عرباتكم وتوفير الكثير مع "أسبوع عروض أبريل" المنتظر في تروليز سوبر ماركت الإمارات! من 23 إلى 30 أبريل 2026، نقدم لكم تشكيلة لا تصدق من العروض على المنتجات الطازجة، المواد الغذائية الأساسية، الأدوات المنزلية، وأكثر من ذلك بكثير. لا تفوتوا هذه الخصومات الأسبوعية الرائعة المصممة لجعل تسوقكم أكثر اقتصادية وممتعة.

سواء كنتم في مردف دبي، التعاون الشارقة، الخان الشارقة، أو النعيمية عجمان، أبوابنا مفتوحة لمساعدتكم في اكتشاف صفقات البقالة المذهلة. لقد قام فريقنا المتفاني بتنظيم كتالوج مليء بالقيمة، مما يضمن حصولكم على الأفضل بأقل الأسعار. زوروا أقرب فرع لتروليز سوبر ماركت اليوم واختبروا فرق الجودة والتوفير!

س: ما هي تواريخ أسبوع عروض أبريل في تروليز سوبر ماركت؟
ج: أسبوع عروض أبريل سارٍ من 23 إلى 30 أبريل 2026، في جميع فروعنا بالإمارات.

س: أين يمكنني العثور على كتالوج عروض أسبوع أبريل؟
ج: يمكنكم الحصول على كتالوج مطبوع في أي من فروعنا أو متابعة قنواتنا على وسائل التواصل الاجتماعي للحصول على التحديثات الرقمية.

posts.excerpt: Discover amazing weekly deals at Trolleys Supermarket UAE. Valid 2026-04-23 across Dubai, Sharjah and Ajman.

posts.title: Healthy Eating in Dubai: Affordable Supermarket Foods for a Balanced Diet
posts.excerpt: Eating healthy in Dubai doesn't have to be expensive. Learn how to build a balanced diet using affordable supermarket foods available at Trolleys across UAE.
posts.content:
Healthy eating is one of the most common goals among UAE residents — yet many people assume it requires expensive specialty stores or imported organic products. The truth is that a nutritious, balanced diet is entirely achievable on a regular supermarket budget.

Why Healthy Eating Matters in the UAE

The UAE has one of the highest rates of lifestyle-related health conditions in the region, including diabetes, obesity and cardiovascular disease. Diet plays a central role in preventing and managing these conditions. The good news is that the building blocks of a healthy diet — vegetables, fruits, wholegrains, lean proteins and dairy — are all available at your local Trolleys supermarket at everyday prices.

The Foundation of a Balanced Diet

A balanced diet doesn't require exotic superfoods or expensive supplements. It simply means eating a variety of foods from each of the main food groups every day.

Fresh vegetables and fruits should make up the largest portion of your diet. At Trolleys, our fresh produce section is stocked daily with seasonal and imported varieties at competitive prices. Aim for at least five portions of fruits and vegetables per day.

Lean proteins — chicken, fish, eggs and legumes — provide the building blocks for muscle, immune function and energy. Trolleys carries a wide range of fresh and frozen protein options to suit every budget.

Wholegrains such as brown rice, oats and wholemeal bread provide sustained energy and fibre. These are widely available and often more affordable than their refined counterparts.

Dairy products including milk, yoghurt and cheese provide calcium and protein. Trolleys stocks a full range of local and imported dairy options.

Affordable Healthy Foods at Trolleys

Oats: One of the most nutritious and affordable breakfast options available. A large pack of oats from Trolleys costs a fraction of branded cereals and provides sustained energy throughout the morning.

Eggs: A complete protein source that is highly affordable and versatile. Use them boiled, scrambled, poached or in omelettes.

Frozen vegetables: Just as nutritious as fresh vegetables, frozen options are often cheaper and have a much longer shelf life. Trolleys carries a wide range of frozen peas, corn, mixed vegetables and more.

Canned legumes: Chickpeas, lentils and kidney beans are excellent sources of plant-based protein and fibre. They are cheap, filling and incredibly versatile.

Bananas: One of the most affordable fruits available year-round, bananas provide potassium, fibre and natural energy.

Healthy Eating Tips for UAE Residents

Meal prep on weekends: Spend a couple of hours on Thursday or Friday preparing meals for the week ahead. This reduces reliance on expensive takeaways and processed foods.

Cook at home more often: Restaurant meals in Dubai can easily cost AED 50 to AED 150 per person. Cooking the same meal at home using ingredients from Trolleys typically costs AED 10 to AED 20.

Choose water over sugary drinks: Cutting out soft drinks and juices is one of the single biggest improvements you can make to your diet. Water is free, and Trolleys carries a wide range of affordable still and sparkling water options.

Read labels: When buying packaged foods, check the nutrition label for sugar, sodium and saturated fat content. Choose options with lower amounts of these.

Frequently Asked Questions

Q: Is it possible to eat healthily on a budget in Dubai?
A: Absolutely. By focusing on whole foods, seasonal produce and store-brand options, a healthy diet is very achievable on a regular supermarket budget.

Q: Does Trolleys carry organic products?
A: Yes, Trolleys stocks a selection of organic products across our stores. Check our offers page for current promotions on organic items.

Q: What are the healthiest foods available at Trolleys?
A: Fresh fruits and vegetables, eggs, legumes, wholegrains, fish and low-fat dairy products are among the most nutritious options available at Trolleys at everyday prices.

Q: Does Trolleys offer delivery for healthy food items?
A: Yes, Trolleys offers home delivery across Dubai, Sharjah and Ajman. Order fresh, healthy groceries and have them delivered to your door.

posts.title: How to Choose Fresh Meat, Fruits & Vegetables in UAE Supermarkets
posts.excerpt: Not sure how to pick the freshest produce at your UAE supermarket? This complete guide teaches you exactly what to look for when choosing meat, fruits and vegetables.
posts.content:
Choosing the freshest ingredients is the foundation of good cooking and good health. Whether you're selecting a piece of chicken, picking out tomatoes or choosing a watermelon, knowing what to look for can make a significant difference to the quality of your meals.

How to Choose Fresh Meat

Colour is one of the most important indicators of fresh meat. Fresh beef should be bright red, fresh chicken should be pale pink and fresh fish should have clear, bright eyes and firm flesh. Avoid any meat with grey or brown discolouration, as this indicates age or improper storage.

Smell is equally important. Fresh meat should have a mild, neutral smell. Any sour, ammonia-like or strong odour is a sign that the meat is no longer fresh.

Texture matters too. Fresh meat should feel firm and spring back when pressed gently. Avoid meat that feels slimy or leaves an indentation when pressed.

At Trolleys, our meat section is restocked daily with fresh cuts from trusted suppliers. Our staff are always available to help you select the best piece for your needs.

How to Choose Fresh Fruits

Different fruits have different indicators of freshness and ripeness.

Watermelon: Look for a yellow spot on the underside — this indicates the melon ripened on the ground. A ripe watermelon should feel heavy for its size and produce a hollow sound when tapped.

Mangoes: A ripe mango should yield slightly to gentle pressure and have a fruity aroma near the stem. Avoid mangoes with large black spots or a fermented smell.

Bananas: Choose bananas based on how quickly you plan to eat them. Green bananas will ripen over several days, while yellow bananas with brown spots are at peak sweetness.

Citrus fruits: Look for fruits that feel heavy for their size — this indicates juiciness. The skin should be smooth and firm, without soft spots.

Strawberries: Choose bright red berries with fresh green caps. Avoid any with white or green patches near the stem or signs of mould.

How to Choose Fresh Vegetables

Leafy greens: Look for vibrant, crisp leaves with no yellowing, wilting or slimy patches. Spinach, lettuce and coriander should look perky and smell fresh.

Tomatoes: A good tomato should be firm but yield slightly to pressure. It should smell tomato-like near the stem. Avoid tomatoes with cracks, soft spots or wrinkled skin.

Cucumbers: Choose firm, dark green cucumbers without soft spots or yellowing. A fresh cucumber should feel solid from end to end.

Onions and garlic: Look for firm, dry outer skins without soft spots or sprouting. Any sprouting indicates age.

Potatoes: Choose firm potatoes without green patches, sprouts or soft spots. Green patches on potatoes contain solanine and should always be removed before cooking.

Storage Tips for Fresh Produce

In the UAE's hot climate, proper storage is essential to maintaining freshness.

Keep most fruits and vegetables in the refrigerator crisper drawer. Exceptions include bananas, avocados, tomatoes and potatoes, which are best stored at room temperature.

Store fresh herbs in a glass of water in the fridge, like flowers. This can extend their life by up to a week.

Keep meat in the coldest part of your fridge and use within two days, or freeze immediately if not using soon.

Frequently Asked Questions

Q: How often is the fresh produce restocked at Trolleys?
A: Our fresh produce, meat and dairy sections are restocked daily across all Trolleys branches.

Q: Can I ask Trolleys staff to help me choose fresh produce?
A: Absolutely. Our team is always happy to help you find the freshest and best-value products in store.

Q: Does Trolleys source meat from local UAE suppliers?
A: Trolleys works with a range of trusted local and international suppliers to provide the best quality fresh meat across our stores.

Q: How should I store fresh meat from Trolleys at home?
A: Store fresh meat in the coldest part of your refrigerator and use within two days. If you won't be using it within two days, freeze it immediately upon returning home.

posts.title: Daily Life in UAE: How Supermarkets Like Trolleys Make Shopping Easier
posts.excerpt: Living in the UAE comes with unique shopping needs and habits. Discover how Trolleys Supermarket fits into the daily lives of residents across Dubai, Sharjah and Ajman.
posts.content:
Life in the UAE is fast-paced, multicultural and convenience-driven. For the millions of residents who call Dubai, Sharjah and Ajman home, the local supermarket is far more than just a place to buy groceries — it's a central part of daily life.

The UAE Shopper Profile

The UAE is one of the most diverse countries in the world, with residents from over 190 nationalities. This diversity is reflected in supermarket shelves, which stock products from across Asia, Europe, the Middle East, Africa and the Americas.

UAE residents tend to shop frequently — many households visit their local supermarket three to five times per week rather than doing a single large weekly shop. This is partly due to the preference for fresh produce and partly due to the convenience of having well-stocked supermarkets in every neighbourhood.

What UAE Residents Look for in a Supermarket

Convenience is paramount. UAE residents lead busy lives, and the ability to find everything in one place — fresh produce, meat, dairy, bakery, household products and international foods — is highly valued.

Product variety is equally important. With such a diverse population, UAE supermarkets need to stock products that cater to a wide range of culinary traditions, dietary requirements and personal preferences.

Price transparency and value are also key. UAE residents are savvy shoppers who compare prices and actively seek out the best deals. Weekly catalogs and loyalty programmes are well-used features.

How Trolleys Serves the UAE Community

Trolleys Supermarket has built its reputation on three pillars: freshness, value and community.

Freshness: Every Trolleys branch receives fresh deliveries daily. Our produce, meat and dairy sections are restocked every morning to ensure you always have access to the freshest products.

Value: Through our weekly offers catalog and competitive everyday pricing, Trolleys ensures that quality shopping is accessible to everyone — from families on a tight budget to those who simply appreciate good value.

Community: With branches in Dubai Mirdif, Sharjah Al Khan, Sharjah Al Taawun and Ajman Al Nuaimia, Trolleys is a genuine neighbourhood supermarket. We know our customers, understand their needs and work hard to serve them well.

Shopping Habits That Work in the UAE

Shop in the evening or early morning to avoid peak-hour crowds and find the freshest restocked products.

Use the Trolleys WhatsApp Channel to plan your week around current offers before you shop.

Build relationships with store staff — they often know which products are being restocked and when the best fresh deliveries arrive.

Frequently Asked Questions

Q: What are the opening hours of Trolleys stores?
A: Trolleys stores are open from 7am to late, seven days a week. Check our Stores page for specific branch hours.

Q: Does Trolleys cater to different dietary requirements?
A: Yes. Trolleys stocks a wide range of halal, vegetarian, vegan, gluten-free and organic products across all branches.

Q: How can I find my nearest Trolleys branch?
A: Use our Store Locator to find the nearest Trolleys in Dubai, Sharjah or Ajman, complete with directions and contact details.

Q: Does Trolleys offer online shopping or delivery?
A: Yes, Trolleys offers home delivery across Dubai, Sharjah and Ajman. Visit our Delivery page for more information.

posts.title: Top Imported Foods in UAE Supermarkets: What You Can Find at Trolleys
posts.excerpt: The UAE is a global hub for imported foods. Explore the wide range of international products available at Trolleys Supermarket across Dubai, Sharjah and Ajman.
posts.content:
One of the great pleasures of living in the UAE is access to food products from virtually every corner of the world. Whether you're craving a specific brand from your home country or simply want to explore new flavours, UAE supermarkets offer an extraordinary range of imported goods.

Why the UAE is a Hub for Imported Foods

The UAE imports approximately 90 percent of its food requirements, making it one of the world's most import-dependent countries for food. This creates a remarkable diversity of products on supermarket shelves — from Japanese snacks and Indian spices to European cheeses and American breakfast cereals.

Dubai in particular has developed into a major food distribution hub for the wider Middle East and South Asia region. This means products often arrive fresher and in greater variety than in many other markets.

Popular Imported Food Categories at Trolleys

Asian Products: The large South Asian and Southeast Asian expatriate communities in the UAE create strong demand for products from India, Pakistan, Sri Lanka, the Philippines, Indonesia and beyond. At Trolleys, you'll find an extensive selection of Asian cooking essentials including basmati rice, lentils, spices, pickles, chutneys, noodles, sauces and snacks.

European Products: European food products are highly popular among UAE residents, particularly dairy items, chocolates, biscuits, pasta, olive oil and coffee. Trolleys stocks a curated range of European brands across these categories.

Middle Eastern and Levantine Products: Despite being in the region, many traditional Middle Eastern products are produced and imported from Lebanon, Jordan, Egypt and Turkey. Trolleys carries a wide range of these products including tahini, za'atar, freekeh, sumac, halva and specialty breads.

African Products: With a growing African expatriate community in the UAE, demand for African food products has increased significantly. Trolleys responds to local community needs across all branches.

American and International Snacks: Imported snacks, cereals and confectionery from the United States and other Western markets are perennially popular. These products are widely available at Trolleys.

How to Find Specific Imported Products

If you're looking for a specific imported product and can't find it on the shelf, don't hesitate to ask a Trolleys team member. Our staff are knowledgeable about our product range and can often help you locate what you're looking for or advise on similar alternatives.

Trolleys also regularly introduces new imported products based on customer feedback and community demand. If there's a product you'd like to see on our shelves, let us know through our contact page.

Frequently Asked Questions

Q: Does Trolleys stock halal-certified imported products?
A: Yes. All meat and poultry products at Trolleys are halal-certified. Many other imported products also carry halal certification — look for the halal logo on the packaging.

Q: Can I find products from my home country at Trolleys?
A: Trolleys stocks products from across Asia, Europe, the Middle East, Africa and the Americas. If you're looking for something specific, ask our team or contact us through our website.

Q: Does Trolleys import products directly?
A: Trolleys works with a network of importers and distributors to source products from around the world. Our buying team continuously evaluates new products to meet the needs of our diverse customer base.

Q: Are imported products more expensive than local products at Trolleys?
A: Not necessarily. Many imported products are competitively priced, particularly staple items such as rice, lentils, oil and spices. Trolleys works hard to offer competitive pricing across all product categories including imports.
